title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: 1. High: quote-only posts will now fail. [`post_tweet()`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L82) rejects empty `message`, but [`extract_quote_tweet_id()`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L62) can strip the only URL and return `""`; [`scheduler.py`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L509) then calls `post_tweet("", quote_tweet_id=...)`, raising `ValueError` instead of posting. 2. Medium: multi-tweet posts now lose data. [`extract_quote_tweet_id()`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L58) removes all matched status URLs via `_QUOTE_URL_RE.sub("", text)` but only uses the first tweet ID, so any second/third source tweet disappears from the published post. 3. Low: this change needs regression tests. There’s no coverage for `quote-only`, `multiple quote URLs`, or the widened domain/path matcher in [`_QUOTE_URL_RE`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L15), so these regressions are easy to miss. No obvious security issue in the diff.
